title: Building a dynamic personal site with Antigravity and Gemma 4
summary: Rody Davis demonstrates using Google Antigravity with Gemma to build a serverless, VS Code-styled website generator. The site features theme switching and offline vector search, using Gemma for post summarization and embedding-gemma for compile-time vectorization to enable dynamic recommendations entirely offline.
writeup: Rody Davis builds a serverless personal website generator using Antigravity and Gemma, with theme switching and offline vector search. The build runs Gemma to summarize posts and uses embedding-gemma to vectorize content at compile time, so the resulting static site supports dynamic, offline recommendations without a backend. The approach matters because it moves summarization and embedding into the build step, keeping inference local and search functional offline. Key takeaways: compile-time embeddings enable static sites with dynamic behavior, and local Gemma inference handles both summarization and content vectorization.
